sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Level 2 has:
- Top-left: black rectangle with 4 colored 2x2 squares (S, M, p, Y)
- A yellow 4x4 square at (3-6, 30-33)
- A blue circular/blob object at (23-29, 31-37)
- 7 sky blue (S) single-pixel dots scattered around
- Bottom: white bar
The colored squares in the top-left might be color selectors. The sky blue dots are the ones I need to interact with.
Let me try clicking on one of the sky blue dots to see what happens. Maybe clicking a dot moves it toward the blue object, or changes its color, or something else.
Let me try clicking on the sky blue dot at (37, 18) first.

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on the first sky blue dot at (37, 18)
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 37, 'col': 18}])

print("Board changed:", last_action_result.get('board_changed'))

# Check what changed

print("\nSky blue dots now:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'S':
        print(f"  id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
